behaviors.description: Required patient fields (name, date of birth, insurance ID) are validated before any downstream processing and missing fields produce a clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "before any downstream processing" and "error listing all missing items." Slot B: decisive boundary is handoff/orchestration gate between validation and downstream modules. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, you could miss a real bug where validation works but insurance/triage/scheduling are still invoked. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model-output or multi-agentic step dependency is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake service with missing required fields using wired module fakes/spies for insurance, triage, scheduling, and confirmation; assert aggregated missing-field error and assert zero downstream invocations.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates more than 150 years ago, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "Date of birth validation rejects future dates...150 years ago...non-date strings." Slot B: decisive boundary is local parsing/range logic in one validator function. Slot C: if lowered below Unit, no executable check exists; Unit already catches the real failures. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because no module handoff is required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests of DOB validator function with valid/invalid inputs and deterministic expected error codes/messages.

behaviors.description: The insurance eligibility check sends the correct patient ID and plan code to the payer API and correctly interprets `eligible` vs `not eligible` vs `pending`.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "sends the correct patient ID and plan code" and "correctly interprets eligible/not eligible/pending." Slot B: decisive boundary is module handoff between eligibility service and payer client/response mapper. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, you can miss serialization/mapping mismatches across request builder and response interpreter. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior does not require real network behavior, only correct payload/interpretation semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + payer client adapter against a stub transport; assert outbound payload fields and mapped internal status for each payer response variant.

behaviors.description: When the payer API is unreachable, the system marks eligibility as `pending` and continues the intake rather than blocking.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "payer API is unreachable" and "continues the intake rather than blocking." Slot B: decisive boundary is handoff/error propagation from payer client failure into intake orchestration fallback. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, you may miss that intake pipeline still aborts despite correct fallback function logic. Rejection gate: rejected System because this behavior can be triggered by deterministic injected transport failure without requiring real network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Use wired intake pipeline with payer client stub that raises timeout/connection errors; assert eligibility becomes pending and downstream triage/scheduling/confirmation still execute.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation includes the reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric in the model input payload.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "includes the reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric in the model input payload." Slot B: decisive boundary is handoff from intake domain model/config to model-client request payload. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, you may miss wiring bugs where config or symptoms are dropped before client call. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior checks payload construction, not model decision quality; rejected System because real network is unnecessary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute triage service with real config loader/request builder wired to a stub model client; capture outbound payload and assert symptoms + rubric are present and correctly keyed.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage assigns high urgency to symptom combinations that indicate emergency conditions (for example chest pain plus shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "live AI symptom triage assigns high urgency" for emergency symptom combinations. Slot B: decisive boundary is real model-output quality/judgment. Slot C: if lowered to Integration, stubbed outputs can always pass and hide failures where the live model under-triages emergencies. Rejection gate: rejected Workflow because behavior is a single model decision, not multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated live triage calls on emergency symptom cases; assert urgency is high with acceptance threshold over multiple runs to account for model variance.

behaviors.description: The triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field (not only a numeric score).
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field." Slot B: decisive boundary is handoff from parser output into persistence schema/record writer. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, parser might produce explanation but persistence mapping could still drop it. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior is about field propagation/storage, not explanation quality from live model.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative triage response payloads through parser + repository layer and assert persisted record contains non-empty explanation column alongside numeric urgency.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "live AI ... explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate." Slot B: decisive boundary is real model-output content quality. Slot C: if lowered to Integration, fixed fixtures cannot reveal generic or non-specific live explanations. Rejection gate: rejected Workflow because this is one agentic generation quality check, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Call live triage on varied symptom sets; evaluate explanation text for mention of input-specific symptoms and explicit rationale patterns, failing generic/template-only responses.

behaviors.description: An appointment is scheduled in the first available slot that matches the assigned urgency level: high urgency gets same-day, medium gets within 48 hours.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "first available slot" and urgency SLA rules (same-day, within 48 hours). Slot B: decisive boundary is handoff between urgency output and scheduling/query logic over calendar data. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, you can miss bugs in repository ordering/filtering that violate SLA despite correct pure algorithm. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior can be validated with local in-process calendar DB/module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: With seeded local calendar DB, run scheduler end-to-end from urgency input and assert chosen slot is earliest valid one meeting each urgency window.

behaviors.description: The scheduling step does not double-book a time slot even when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "does not double-book ... when two patients are processed concurrently." Slot B: decisive boundary is concurrent handoff/transaction interaction between scheduler service and shared calendar DB. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, race conditions in locking/constraints are hidden. Rejection gate: rejected System because real external OS/network is not required if concurrency + real local DB constraints are exercised in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Start two concurrent intake/scheduling transactions targeting overlapping availability against the real local calendar database schema; assert only one booking succeeds for the contested slot.

behaviors.description: The confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and preparation instructions appropriate to visit type.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and preparation instructions appropriate to visit type." Slot B: decisive boundary is data handoff from patient/schedule/visit-type modules into confirmation template/composer. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, template logic may pass while upstream field mapping omissions still break real messages. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because no model output quality is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run confirmation composer with real mapping layer from intake/schedule entities and assert rendered message contains all required fields and visit-type-specific instructions.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "Pipeline orchestration ... completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output." Slot B: decisive boundary is multi-module orchestration/state handoff across pipeline stages, explicitly non-live model. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, cross-stage sequencing and state propagation bugs are missed. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text explicitly disqualifies live model dependence via "deterministic stubbed triage output."
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline with deterministic triage stub and stubbed payer responses in-process; assert stage order, shared state transitions, and final artifacts (scheduled appointment + confirmation).

behaviors.description: The full live intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "full live intake pipeline" with "live ... triage" for a "real patient submission." Slot B: decisive boundary is real model-output dependency inside end-to-end orchestration. Slot C: if lowered to Integration, stubbed triage cannot catch failures where live model output format/content breaks downstream stages. Rejection gate: rejected Workflow because behavior specifies one agentic stage (triage) within the pipeline, not multiple sequential agentic decision stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end intake against live payer integration (or production-like endpoint) and live triage model for real submission data; assert automatic completion through confirmation with no operator steps.
